# Code Citations

## License: unknown
https://github.com/0759675781/API/blob/84e8070ce513339498f2bc3c1d2d1c3b9dec3b67/models/User.js

```


Here's a complete onion architecture for your dating app API:

## Folder Structure

```
dating-app-api/
│
├── server.js                  # Entry point: starts Express server
├── syncDatabase.js            # DB creation & table sync utility
├── package.json
├── .env
│
└── src/
    │
    ├── config/
    │   └── database.js        # Sequelize instance (innermost layer - no dependencies)
    │
    ├── models/                # Domain/Entity layer
    │   ├── index.js           # Central hub: registers all models + associations
    │   ├── userModel.js       # User entity definition
    │   ├── profileModel.js    # Profile entity definition
    │   ├── matchModel.js      # Match entity definition
    │   └── messageModel.js    # Message entity definition
    │
    ├── repositories/          # Data Access layer (talks to models only)
    │   ├── userRepository.js  # CRUD operations for User
    │   ├── profileRepository.js
    │   ├── matchRepository.js
    │   └── messageRepository.js
    │
    ├── services/              # Business Logic layer (talks to repositories only)
    │   ├── authService.js     # Login, register, token logic
    │   ├── userService.js     # User-related business rules
    │   ├── profileService.js  # Profile business rules
    │   ├── matchService.js    # Matching algorithm & rules
    │   └── messageService.js  # Messaging business rules
    │
    ├── controllers/           # Interface/Adapter layer (talks to services only)
    │   ├── authController.js  # Handles auth HTTP requests
    │   ├── userController.js  # Handles user HTTP requests
    │   ├── profileController.js
    │   ├── matchController.js
    │   └── messageController.js
    │
    ├── routes/                # Route definitions (maps URLs to controllers)
    │   ├── index.js           # Central route hub
    │   ├── authRoutes.js
    │   ├── userRoutes.js
    │   ├── profileRoutes.js
    │   ├── matchRoutes.js
    │   └── messageRoutes.js
    │
    ├── middlewares/            # Cross-cutting concerns
    │   ├── authMiddleware.js  # JWT verification
    │   ├── errorHandler.js    # Global error handler
    │   └── validator.js       # Request validation
    │
    └── utils/                 # Shared helpers
        ├── apiResponse.js     # Standardized API responses
        ├── constants.js       # App-wide constants
        └── helpers.js         # Utility functions
```

## Dependency Flow (Onion Layers)

```
Outermost ──────────────────────────────────── Innermost

Routes → Controllers → Services → Repositories → Models → Config/DB
  │           │            │            │            │         │
  │           │            │            │            │         └─ Pure Sequelize instance
  │           │            │            │            └─ Entity definitions (schema)
  │           │            │            └─ Raw DB queries (findAll, create, etc.)
  │           │            └─ Business rules & validation
  │           └─ HTTP req/res handling
  └─ URL mapping
```

> **Rule:** Each layer only imports from the layer directly inside it. Never skip layers.

## Example Files

### 1. Config (Innermost)

````javascript
// filepath: c:\Users\Anand\Documents\Laravel\dating-app-api\src\config\database.js
const { Sequelize } = require('sequelize');
require('dotenv').config();

const sequelize = new Sequelize(
    process.env.DB_NAME || 'dating_app',
    process.env.DB_USER || 'root',
    process.env.DB_PASSWORD || '',
    {
        host: process.env.DB_HOST || 'localhost',
        dialect: 'mysql',
        logging: false,
        timezone: '+05:30',
        pool: { max: 5, min: 0, acquire: 30000, idle: 10000 }
    }
);

module.exports = sequelize;
````

### 2. Model Layer

````javascript
// filepath: c:\Users\Anand\Documents\Laravel\dating-app-api\src\models\userModel.js
const { DataTypes } = require('sequelize');
const sequelize = require('../config/database');

const User = sequelize.define('User', {
    id: {
        type: DataTypes.INTEGER,
        autoIncrement: true,
        primaryKey: true,
    },
    name: {
        type: DataTypes
```

  │           │            │            │            │         └─ Pure Sequelize instance
  │           │            │            │            └─ Entity definitions (schema)
  │           │            │            └─ Raw DB queries (findAll, create, etc.)
  │           │            └─ Business rules & validation
  │           └─ HTTP req/res handling
  └─ URL mapping
```

> **Rule:** Each layer only imports from the layer directly inside it. Never skip layers.

## Example Files

### 1. Config (Innermost)

````javascript
// filepath: c:\Users\Anand\Documents\Laravel\dating-app-api\src\config\database.js
const { Sequelize } = require('sequelize');
require('dotenv').config();

const sequelize = new Sequelize(
    process.env.DB_NAME || 'dating_app',
    process.env.DB_USER || 'root',
    process.env.DB_PASSWORD || '',
    {
        host: process.env.DB_HOST || 'localhost',
        dialect: 'mysql',
        logging: false,
        timezone: '+05:30',
        pool: { max: 5, min: 0, acquire: 30000, idle: 10000 }
    }
);

module.exports = sequelize;
````

### 2. Model Layer

````javascript
// filepath: c:\Users\Anand\Documents\Laravel\dating-app-api\src\models\userModel.js

const sequelize = require('../config/database');

const User = sequelize.define('User', {
    id: {
        type: DataTypes.INTEGER,
        autoIncrement: true,
        primaryKey: true,
    },
    name: {
        type: DataTypes.STRING,
        allowNull: false,
    },
    email: {
        type: DataTypes.STRING,
        allowNull:

Here's a complete onion architecture for your dating app API:

## Folder Structure

```
dating-app-api/
│
├── server.js                  # Entry point: starts Express server
├── syncDatabase.js            # DB creation & table sync utility
├── package.json
├── .env
│
└── src/
    │
    ├── config/
    │   └── database.js        # Sequelize instance (innermost layer - no dependencies)
    │
    ├── models/                # Domain/Entity layer
    │   ├── index.js           # Central hub: registers all models + associations
    │   ├── userModel.js       # User entity definition
    │   ├── profileModel.js    # Profile entity definition
    │   ├── matchModel.js      # Match entity definition
    │   └── messageModel.js    # Message entity definition
    │
    ├── repositories/          # Data Access layer (talks to models only)
    │   ├── userRepository.js  # CRUD operations for User
    │   ├── profileRepository.js
    │   ├── matchRepository.js
    │   └── messageRepository.js
    │
    ├── services/              # Business Logic layer (talks to repositories only)
    │   ├── authService.js     # Login, register, token logic
    │   ├── userService.js     # User-related business rules
    │   ├── profileService.js  # Profile business rules
    │   ├── matchService.js    # Matching algorithm & rules
    │   └── messageService.js  # Messaging business rules
    │
    ├── controllers/           # Interface/Adapter layer (talks to services only)
    │   ├── authController.js  # Handles auth HTTP requests
    │   ├── userController.js  # Handles user HTTP requests
    │   ├── profileController.js
    │   ├── matchController.js
    │   └── messageController.js
    │
    ├── routes/                # Route definitions (maps URLs to controllers)
    │   ├── index.js           # Central route hub
    │   ├── authRoutes.js
    │   ├── userRoutes.js
    │   ├── profileRoutes.js
    │   ├── matchRoutes.js
    │   └── messageRoutes.js
    │
    ├── middlewares/            # Cross-cutting concerns
    │   ├── authMiddleware.js  # JWT verification
    │   ├── errorHandler.js    # Global error handler
    │   └── validator.js       # Request validation
    │
    └── utils/                 # Shared helpers
        ├── apiResponse.js     # Standardized API responses
        ├── constants.js       # App-wide constants
        └── helpers.js         # Utility functions
```

Outermost ──────────────────────────────────── Innermost

  │           │            │            │            │         │
  │           │            │            │            │         └─ Pure Sequelize instance
  │           │            │            │            └─ Entity definitions (schema)
  │           │            │            └─ Raw DB queries (findAll, create, etc.)
  │           │            └─ Business rules & validation
  │           └─ HTTP req/res handling
  └─ URL mapping
```

    │   ├── userModel.js       # User entity definition
    │   ├── profileModel.js    # Profile entity definition
    │   ├── matchModel.js      # Match entity definition
    │   └── messageModel.js    # Message entity definition
    │
    ├── repositories/          # Data Access layer (talks to models only)
    │   ├── userRepository.js  # CRUD operations for User
    │   ├── profileRepository.js
    │   ├── matchRepository.js
    │   └── messageRepository.js
    │
    ├── services/              # Business Logic layer (talks to repositories only)
    │   ├── authService.js     # Login, register, token logic
    │   ├── userService.js     # User-related business rules
    │   ├── profileService.js  # Profile business rules
    │   ├── matchService.js    # Matching algorithm & rules
    │   └── messageService.js  # Messaging business rules
    │
    ├── controllers/           # Interface/Adapter layer (talks to services only)
    │   ├── authController.js  # Handles auth HTTP requests
    │   ├── userController.js  # Handles user HTTP requests
    │   ├── profileController.js
    │   ├── matchController.js
    │   └── messageController.js
    │
    ├── routes/                # Route definitions (maps URLs to controllers)
    │   ├── index.js           # Central route hub
    │   ├── authRoutes.js
    │   ├── userRoutes.js
    │   ├── profileRoutes.js
    │   ├── matchRoutes.js
    │   └── messageRoutes.js
    │
    ├── middlewares/            # Cross-cutting concerns
    │   ├── authMiddleware.js  # JWT verification
    │   ├── errorHandler.js    # Global error handler
    │   └── validator.js       # Request validation
    │
    └── utils/                 # Shared helpers
        ├── apiResponse.js     # Standardized API responses
        ├── constants.js       # App-wide constants
        └── helpers.js         # Utility functions
```

## Dependency Flow (Onion Layers)

```
Outermost ──────────────────────────────────── Innermost

Routes → Controllers → Services → Repositories → Models → Config/DB
  │           │            │            │            │         │
  │           │            │            │            │         └─ Pure Sequelize instance
  │           │            │            │            └─ Entity definitions (schema)
  │           │            │            └─ Raw DB queries (findAll, create, etc.)
  │           │            └─ Business rules & validation
  │           └─ HTTP req/res handling
  └─ URL mapping
```

> **Rule:** Each layer only imports from the layer directly inside it. Never skip layers.

## Example Files

### 1. Config (Innermost)

````javascript
// filepath: c:\Users\Anand\Documents\Laravel\dating-app-api\src\config\database.js
const { Sequelize } = require('sequelize');
require('dotenv').config();

const sequelize = new Sequelize(
    process.env.DB_NAME || 'dating_app',
    process.env.DB_USER || 'root',
    process.env.DB_PASSWORD || '',
    {
        host: process.env.DB_HOST || 'localhost',
        dialect: 'mysql',
        logging: false,
        timezone: '+05:30',
        pool: { max: 5, min: 0, acquire: 30000, idle: 10000 }
    }
);

module.exports = sequelize;
````

  │           │            │            │            │         └─ Pure Sequelize instance
  │           │            │            │            └─ Entity definitions (schema)
  │           │            │            └─ Raw DB queries (findAll, create, etc.)
  │           │            └─ Business rules & validation
  │           └─ HTTP req/res handling
  └─ URL mapping
```

> **Rule:** Each layer only imports from the layer directly inside it. Never skip layers.

## Example Files

### 1. Config (Innermost)

````javascript
// filepath: c:\Users\Anand\Documents\Laravel\dating-app-api\src\config\database.js
const { Sequelize } = require('sequelize');
require('dotenv').config();

const sequelize = new Sequelize(
    process.env.DB_NAME || 'dating_app',
    process.env.DB_USER || 'root',
    process.env.DB_PASSWORD || '',
    {
        host: process.env.DB_HOST || 'localhost',
        dialect: 'mysql',
        logging: false,
        timezone: '+05:30',
        pool: { max: 5, min: 0, acquire: 30000, idle: 10000 }
    }
);

module.exports = sequelize;
````

### 2. Model Layer

````javascript
// filepath: c:\Users\Anand\Documents\Laravel\dating-app-api\src\models\userModel.js
const { DataTypes } = require('sequelize');
const sequelize = require('../config/database');

const User = sequelize.define('User', {
    id: {
        type: DataTypes.INTEGER,
        autoIncrement: true,
        primaryKey: true,
    },
    name: {
        type: DataTypes.STRING,
        allowNull: false,
    },
    email: {
        type: DataTypes.STRING,
        allowNull: false,
        unique: true,
    },
    password: {
        type: DataTypes.STRING,
        allowNull: false,
    },
    phone
```

Here's a complete onion architecture for your dating app API:

## Folder Structure

```
dating-app-api/
│
├── server.js                  # Entry point: starts Express server
├── syncDatabase.js            # DB creation & table sync utility
├── package.json
├── .env
│
└── src/
    │
    ├── config/
    │   └── database.js        # Sequelize instance (innermost layer - no dependencies)
    │
    ├── models/                # Domain/Entity layer
    │   ├── index.js           # Central hub: registers all models + associations
    │   ├── userModel.js       # User entity definition
    │   ├── profileModel.js    # Profile entity definition
    │   ├── matchModel.js      # Match entity definition
    │   └── messageModel.js    # Message entity definition
    │
    ├── repositories/          # Data Access layer (talks to models only)
    │   ├── userRepository.js  # CRUD operations for User
    │   ├── profileRepository.js
    │   ├── matchRepository.js
    │   └── messageRepository.js
    │
    ├── services/              # Business Logic layer (talks to repositories only)
    │   ├── authService.js     # Login, register, token logic
    │   ├── userService.js     # User-related business rules
    │   ├── profileService.js  # Profile business rules
    │   ├── matchService.js    # Matching algorithm & rules
    │   └── messageService.js  # Messaging business rules
    │
    ├── controllers/           # Interface/Adapter layer (talks to services only)
    │   ├── authController.js  # Handles auth HTTP requests
    │   ├── userController.js  # Handles user HTTP requests
    │   ├── profileController.js
    │   ├── matchController.js
    │   └── messageController.js
    │
    ├── routes/                # Route definitions (maps URLs to controllers)
    │   ├── index.js           # Central route hub
    │   ├── authRoutes.js
    │   ├── userRoutes.js
    │   ├── profileRoutes.js
    │   ├── matchRoutes.js
    │   └── messageRoutes.js
    │
    ├── middlewares/            # Cross-cutting concerns
    │   ├── authMiddleware.js  # JWT verification
    │   ├── errorHandler.js    # Global error handler
    │   └── validator.js       # Request validation
    │
    └── utils/                 # Shared helpers
        ├── apiResponse.js     # Standardized API responses
        ├── constants.js       # App-wide constants
        └── helpers.js         # Utility functions
```

└── src/
    │
    ├── config/
    │   └── database.js        # Sequelize instance (innermost layer - no dependencies)
    │
    ├── models/                # Domain/Entity layer
    │   ├── index.js           # Central hub: registers all models + associations
    │   ├── userModel.js       # User entity definition
    │   ├── profileModel.js    # Profile entity definition
    │   ├── matchModel.js      # Match entity definition
    │   └── messageModel.js    # Message entity definition
    │
    ├── repositories/          # Data Access layer (talks to models only)
    │   ├── userRepository.js  # CRUD operations for User
    │   ├── profileRepository.js
    │   ├── matchRepository.js
    │   └── messageRepository.js
    │
    ├── services/              # Business Logic layer (talks to repositories only)
    │   ├── authService.js     # Login, register, token logic
    │   ├── userService.js     # User-related business rules
    │   ├── profileService.js  # Profile business rules
    │   ├── matchService.js    # Matching algorithm & rules
    │   └── messageService.js  # Messaging business rules
    │
    ├── controllers/           # Interface/Adapter layer (talks to services only)
    │   ├── authController.js  # Handles auth HTTP requests
    │   ├── userController.js  # Handles user HTTP requests
    │   ├── profileController.js
    │   ├── matchController.js
    │   └── messageController.js
    │
    ├── routes/                # Route definitions (maps URLs to controllers)
    │   ├── index.js           # Central route hub
    │   ├── authRoutes.js
    │   ├── userRoutes.js
    │   ├── profileRoutes.js
    │   ├── matchRoutes.js
    │   └── messageRoutes.js
    │
    ├── middlewares/            # Cross-cutting concerns
    │   ├── authMiddleware.js  # JWT verification
    │   ├── errorHandler.js    # Global error handler
    │   └── validator.js       # Request validation
    │
    └── utils/                 # Shared helpers
        ├── apiResponse.js     # Standardized API responses
        ├── constants.js       # App-wide constants
        └── helpers.js         # Utility functions
```

## Dependency Flow (Onion Layers)

```
Outermost ──────────────────────────────────── Innermost

Routes → Controllers → Services → Repositories → Models → Config/DB
  │           │            │            │            │         │
  │           │            │            │            │         └─ Pure Sequelize instance
  │           │            │            │            └─ Entity definitions (schema)
  │           │            │            └─ Raw DB queries (findAll, create, etc.)
  │           │            └─ Business rules & validation
  │           └─ HTTP req/res handling
  └─ URL mapping
```

> **Rule:** Each layer only imports from the layer directly inside it. Never skip layers.

## Example Files

### 1. Config (Innermost)

````javascript
// filepath: c:\Users\Anand\Documents\Laravel\dating-app-api\src\config\database.js
const { Sequelize } = require('sequelize');
require('dotenv').config();

const sequelize = new Sequelize(
    process.env.DB_NAME || 'dating_app',
    process.env.DB_USER || 'root',
    process.env.DB_PASSWORD || '',
    {
        host: process.env.DB_HOST || 'localhost',
        dialect: 'mysql',
        logging: false,
        timezone: '+05:30',
        pool: { max: 5, min: 0, acquire: 30000, idle: 10000 }
    }
);

module.exports = sequelize;
````

### 2. Model Layer

````javascript
// filepath: c:\Users\Anand\Documents\Laravel\dating-app-api\src\models\userModel.js
const { DataTypes } = require('sequelize');
const sequelize = require('../config/database');

const User = sequelize.define('User', {
    id: {
        type: DataTypes.INTEGER,
        autoIncrement: true,
        primaryKey: true,
    },
    name: {
        type: DataTypes.STRING,
        allowNull: false,
    },
    email: {
        type: DataTypes.STRING,
        allowNull: false,
        unique: true,
    },
    password: {
        type: DataTypes.STRING,
        allowNull: false,
    },
    phone
```

  │           │            │            │            │         │
  │           │            │            │            │         └─ Pure Sequelize instance
  │           │            │            │            └─ Entity definitions (schema)
  │           │            │            └─ Raw DB queries (findAll, create, etc.)
  │           │            └─ Business rules & validation
  │           └─ HTTP req/res handling
  └─ URL mapping
```

> **Rule:** Each layer only imports from the layer directly inside it. Never skip layers.

## Example Files

### 1. Config (Innermost)

````javascript
// filepath: c:\Users\Anand\Documents\Laravel\dating-app-api\src\config\database.js
const { Sequelize } = require('sequelize');
require('dotenv').config();

const sequelize = new Sequelize(
    process.env.DB_NAME || 'dating_app',
    process.env.DB_USER || 'root',
    process.env.DB_PASSWORD || '',
    {
        host: process.env.DB_HOST || 'localhost',
        dialect: 'mysql',
        logging: false,
        timezone: '+05:30',
        pool: { max: 5, min: 0, acquire: 30000, idle: 10000 }
    }
);

module.exports = sequelize;
````

### 2. Model Layer

````javascript
// filepath: c:\Users\Anand\Documents\Laravel\dating-app-api\src\models\userModel.js
const { DataTypes } = require('sequelize');
const sequelize = require('../config/database');

const User = sequelize.define('User', {
    id: {
        type: DataTypes.INTEGER,
        autoIncrement: true,
        primaryKey: true,
    },
    name: {
        type: DataTypes.STRING,
        allowNull: false,
    },
    email: {
        type: DataTypes.STRING,
        allowNull: false,
        unique: true,
    },
    password: {
        type: DataTypes.STRING,
        allowNull: false,
    },
    phone
```

Here's a complete onion architecture for your dating app API:

## Folder Structure

```
dating-app-api/
│
├── server.js                  # Entry point: starts Express server
├── syncDatabase.js            # DB creation & table sync utility
├── package.json
├── .env
│
└── src/
    │
    ├── config/
    │   └── database.js        # Sequelize instance (innermost layer - no dependencies)
    │
    ├── models/                # Domain/Entity layer
    │   ├── index.js           # Central hub: registers all models + associations
    │   ├── userModel.js       # User entity definition
    │   ├── profileModel.js    # Profile entity definition
    │   ├── matchModel.js      # Match entity definition
    │   └── messageModel.js    # Message entity definition
    │
    ├── repositories/          # Data Access layer (talks to models only)
    │   ├── userRepository.js  # CRUD operations for User
    │   ├── profileRepository.js
    │   ├── matchRepository.js
    │   └── messageRepository.js
    │
    ├── services/              # Business Logic layer (talks to repositories only)
    │   ├── authService.js     # Login, register, token logic
    │   ├── userService.js     # User-related business rules
    │   ├── profileService.js  # Profile business rules
    │   ├── matchService.js    # Matching algorithm & rules
    │   └── messageService.js  # Messaging business rules
    │
    ├── controllers/           # Interface/Adapter layer (talks to services only)
    │   ├── authController.js  # Handles auth HTTP requests
    │   ├── userController.js  # Handles user HTTP requests
    │   ├── profileController.js
    │   ├── matchController.js
    │   └── messageController.js
    │
    ├── routes/                # Route definitions (maps URLs to controllers)
    │   ├── index.js           # Central route hub
    │   ├── authRoutes.js
    │   ├── userRoutes.js
    │   ├── profileRoutes.js
    │   ├── matchRoutes.js
    │   └── messageRoutes.js
    │
    ├── middlewares/            # Cross-cutting concerns
    │   ├── authMiddleware.js  # JWT verification
    │   ├── errorHandler.js    # Global error handler
    │   └── validator.js       # Request validation
    │
    └── utils/                 # Shared helpers
        ├── apiResponse.js     # Standardized API responses
        ├── constants.js       # App-wide constants
        └── helpers.js         # Utility functions
```

dating-app-api/
│
├── server.js                  # Entry point: starts Express server
├── syncDatabase.js            # DB creation & table sync utility
├── package.json
├── .env
│
└── src/
    │
    ├── config/
    │   └── database.js        # Sequelize instance (innermost layer - no dependencies)
    │
    ├── models/                # Domain/Entity layer
    │   ├── index.js           # Central hub: registers all models + associations
    │   ├── userModel.js       # User entity definition
    │   ├── profileModel.js    # Profile entity definition
    │   ├── matchModel.js      # Match entity definition
    │   └── messageModel.js    # Message entity definition
    │
    ├── repositories/          # Data Access layer (talks to models only)
    │   ├── userRepository.js  # CRUD operations for User
    │   ├── profileRepository.js
    │   ├── matchRepository.js
    │   └── messageRepository.js
    │
    ├── services/              # Business Logic layer (talks to repositories only)
    │   ├── authService.js     # Login, register, token logic
    │   ├── userService.js     # User-related business rules
    │   ├── profileService.js  # Profile business rules
    │   ├── matchService.js    # Matching algorithm & rules
    │   └── messageService.js  # Messaging business rules
    │
    ├── controllers/           # Interface/Adapter layer (talks to services only)
    │   ├── authController.js  # Handles auth HTTP requests
    │   ├── userController.js  # Handles user HTTP requests
    │   ├── profileController.js
    │   ├── matchController.js
    │   └── messageController.js
    │
    ├── routes/                # Route definitions (maps URLs to controllers)
    │   ├── index.js           # Central route hub
    │   ├── authRoutes.js
    │   ├── userRoutes.js
    │   ├── profileRoutes.js
    │   ├── matchRoutes.js
    │   └── messageRoutes.js
    │
    ├── middlewares/            # Cross-cutting concerns
    │   ├── authMiddleware.js  # JWT verification
    │   ├── errorHandler.js    # Global error handler
    │   └── validator.js       # Request validation
    │
    └── utils/                 # Shared helpers
        ├── apiResponse.js     # Standardized API responses
        ├── constants.js       # App-wide constants
        └── helpers.js         # Utility functions
```

## Dependency Flow (Onion Layers)

```
Outermost ──────────────────────────────────── Innermost

Routes → Controllers → Services → Repositories → Models → Config/DB
  │           │            │            │            │         │
  │           │            │            │            │         └─ Pure Sequelize instance
  │           │            │            │            └─ Entity definitions (schema)
  │           │            │            └─ Raw DB queries (findAll, create, etc.)
  │           │            └─ Business rules & validation
  │           └─ HTTP req/res handling
  └─ URL mapping
```

> **Rule:** Each layer only imports from the layer directly inside it. Never skip layers.

## Example Files

### 1. Config (Innermost)

````javascript
// filepath: c:\Users\Anand\Documents\Laravel\dating-app-api\src\config\database.js
const { Sequelize } = require('sequelize');
require('dotenv').config();

const sequelize = new Sequelize(
    process.env.DB_NAME || 'dating_app',
    process.env.DB_USER || 'root',
    process.env.DB_PASSWORD || '',
    {
        host: process.env.DB_HOST || 'localhost',
        dialect: 'mysql',
        logging: false,
        timezone: '+05:30',
        pool: { max: 5, min: 0, acquire: 30000, idle: 10000 }
    }
);

module.exports = sequelize;
````

### 2. Model Layer

````javascript
// filepath: c:\Users\Anand\Documents\Laravel\dating-app-api\src\models\userModel.js
const { DataTypes } = require('sequelize');
const sequelize = require('../config/database');

const User = sequelize.define('User', {
    id: {
        type: DataTypes.INTEGER,
        autoIncrement: true,
        primaryKey: true,
    },
    name: {
        type: DataTypes.STRING,
        allowNull: false,
    },
    email: {
        type: DataTypes.STRING,
        allowNull: false,
        unique: true,
    },
    password: {
        type: DataTypes.STRING,
        allowNull: false,
```

Here's a complete onion architecture for your dating app API:

## Folder Structure

```
dating-app-api/
│
├── server.js                  # Entry point: starts Express server
├── syncDatabase.js            # DB creation & table sync utility
├── package.json
├── .env
│
└── src/
    │
    ├── config/
    │   └── database.js        # Sequelize instance (innermost layer - no dependencies)
    │
    ├── models/                # Domain/Entity layer
    │   ├── index.js           # Central hub: registers all models + associations
    │   ├── userModel.js       # User entity definition
    │   ├── profileModel.js    # Profile entity definition
    │   ├── matchModel.js      # Match entity definition
    │   └── messageModel.js    # Message entity definition
    │
    ├── repositories/          # Data Access layer (talks to models only)
    │   ├── userRepository.js  # CRUD operations for User
    │   ├── profileRepository.js
    │   ├── matchRepository.js
    │   └── messageRepository.js
    │
    ├── services/              # Business Logic layer (talks to repositories only)
    │   ├── authService.js     # Login, register, token logic
    │   ├── userService.js     # User-related business rules
    │   ├── profileService.js  # Profile business rules
    │   ├── matchService.js    # Matching algorithm & rules
    │   └── messageService.js  # Messaging business rules
    │
    ├── controllers/           # Interface/Adapter layer (talks to services only)
    │   ├── authController.js  # Handles auth HTTP requests
    │   ├── userController.js  # Handles user HTTP requests
    │   ├── profileController.js
    │   ├── matchController.js
    │   └── messageController.js
    │
    ├── routes/                # Route definitions (maps URLs to controllers)
    │   ├── index.js           # Central route hub
    │   ├── authRoutes.js
    │   ├── userRoutes.js
    │   ├── profileRoutes.js
    │   ├── matchRoutes.js
    │   └── messageRoutes.js
    │
    ├── middlewares/            # Cross-cutting concerns
    │   ├── authMiddleware.js  # JWT verification
    │   ├── errorHandler.js    # Global error handler
    │   └── validator.js       # Request validation
    │
    └── utils/                 # Shared helpers
        ├── apiResponse.js     # Standardized API responses
        ├── constants.js       # App-wide constants
        └── helpers.js         # Utility functions
```

    ├── routes/                # Route definitions (maps URLs to controllers)
    │   ├── index.js           # Central route hub
    │   ├── authRoutes.js
    │   ├── userRoutes.js

        ├── apiResponse.js     # Standardized API responses

        └── helpers.js         # Utility functions
```

## Dependency Flow (Onion Layers)

```
Outermost ──────────────────────────────────── Innermost

Routes → Controllers → Services → Repositories → Models → Config/DB
  │           │            │            │            │         │
  │           │            │            │            │         └─ Pure Sequelize instance
  │           │            │            │            └─ Entity definitions (schema)
  │           │            │            └─ Raw DB queries (findAll, create, etc.)
  │           │            └─ Business rules & validation
  │           └─ HTTP req/res handling
  └─ URL mapping
```

> **Rule:** Each layer only imports from the layer directly inside it. Never skip layers.

Here's a complete onion architecture for your dating app API:

## Folder Structure

```
dating-app-api/
│
├── server.js                  # Entry point: starts Express server
├── syncDatabase.js            # DB creation & table sync utility
├── package.json
├── .env
│
└── src/
    │
    ├── config/
    │   └── database.js        # Sequelize instance (innermost layer - no dependencies)
    │
    ├── models/                # Domain/Entity layer
    │   ├── index.js           # Central hub: registers all models + associations
    │   ├── userModel.js       # User entity definition
    │   ├── profileModel.js    # Profile entity definition
    │   ├── matchModel.js      # Match entity definition
    │   └── messageModel.js    # Message entity definition
    │
    ├── repositories/          # Data Access layer (talks to models only)
    │   ├── userRepository.js  # CRUD operations for User
    │   ├── profileRepository.js
    │   ├── matchRepository.js
    │   └── messageRepository.js
    │
    ├── services/              # Business Logic layer (talks to repositories only)
    │   ├── authService.js     # Login, register, token logic
    │   ├── userService.js     # User-related business rules
    │   ├── profileService.js  # Profile business rules
    │   ├── matchService.js    # Matching algorithm & rules
    │   └── messageService.js  # Messaging business rules
    │
    ├── controllers/           # Interface/Adapter layer (talks to services only)
    │   ├── authController.js  # Handles auth HTTP requests
    │   ├── userController.js  # Handles user HTTP requests
    │   ├── profileController.js
    │   ├── matchController.js
    │   └── messageController.js
    │
    ├── routes/                # Route definitions (maps URLs to controllers)
    │   ├── index.js           # Central route hub
    │   ├── authRoutes.js
    │   ├── userRoutes.js
    │   ├── profileRoutes.js
    │   ├── matchRoutes.js
    │   └── messageRoutes.js
    │
    ├── middlewares/            # Cross-cutting concerns
    │   ├── authMiddleware.js  # JWT verification
    │   ├── errorHandler.js    # Global error handler
    │   └── validator.js       # Request validation
    │
    └── utils/                 # Shared helpers
        ├── apiResponse.js     # Standardized API responses
        ├── constants.js       # App-wide constants
        └── helpers.js         # Utility functions
```

## Dependency Flow (Onion Layers)

```
Outermost ──────────────────────────────────── Innermost

Routes → Controllers → Services → Repositories → Models → Config/DB
  │           │            │            │            │         │
  │           │            │            │            │         └─ Pure Sequelize instance
  │           │            │            │            └─ Entity definitions (schema)
  │           │            │            └─ Raw DB queries (findAll, create, etc.)
  │           │            └─ Business rules & validation
  │           └─ HTTP req/res handling
  └─ URL mapping
```

> **Rule:** Each layer only imports from the layer directly inside it. Never skip layers.

## Example Files

### 1. Config (Innermost)

````javascript
// filepath: c:\Users\Anand\Documents\Laravel\dating-app-api\src\config\database.js
const { Sequelize } = require('sequelize');
require('dotenv').config();

const sequelize = new Sequelize(
    process.env.DB_NAME || 'dating_app',
    process.env.DB_USER || 'root',
    process.env.DB_PASSWORD || '',
    {
        host: process.env.DB_HOST || 'localhost',
        dialect: 'mysql',
        logging: false,
        timezone: '+05:30',
        pool: { max: 5, min: 0, acquire: 30000, idle: 10000 }
    }
);

module.exports = sequelize;
````

### 2. Model Layer

````javascript
// filepath: c:\Users\Anand\Documents\Laravel\dating-app-api\src\models\userModel.js
const { DataTypes } = require('sequelize');
const sequelize = require('../config/database');

const User = sequelize.define('User', {
    id: {
        type: DataTypes.INTEGER,
        autoIncrement: true,
        primaryKey: true,
    },
    name: {
        type: DataTypes.STRING,
        allowNull: false,
    },
    email: {
        type: DataTypes.STRING,
        allowNull: false,
        unique: true,
    },
    password: {
        type: DataTypes.STRING,
        allowNull: false,
    },
    phone: {
        type: DataTypes.STRING,
        allowNull: true,
    },
    gender
```

    │   ├── profileService.js  # Profile business rules
    │   ├── matchService.js    # Matching algorithm & rules
    │   └── messageService.js  # Messaging business rules
    │
    ├── controllers/           # Interface/Adapter layer (talks to services only)
    │   ├── authController.js  # Handles auth HTTP requests
    │   ├── userController.js  # Handles user HTTP requests
    │   ├── profileController.js
    │   ├── matchController.js
    │   └── messageController.js
    │
    ├── routes/                # Route definitions (maps URLs to controllers)
    │   ├── index.js           # Central route hub
    │   ├── authRoutes.js
    │   ├── userRoutes.js
    │   ├── profileRoutes.js
    │   ├── matchRoutes.js
    │   └── messageRoutes.js
    │
    ├── middlewares/            # Cross-cutting concerns
    │   ├── authMiddleware.js  # JWT verification
    │   ├── errorHandler.js    # Global error handler
    │   └── validator.js       # Request validation
    │
    └── utils/                 # Shared helpers
        ├── apiResponse.js     # Standardized API responses
        ├── constants.js       # App-wide constants
        └── helpers.js         # Utility functions
```

## Dependency Flow (Onion Layers)

```
Outermost ──────────────────────────────────── Innermost

Routes → Controllers → Services → Repositories → Models → Config/DB
  │           │            │            │            │         │
  │           │            │            │            │         └─ Pure Sequelize instance
  │           │            │            │            └─ Entity definitions (schema)
  │           │            │            └─ Raw DB queries (findAll, create, etc.)
  │           │            └─ Business rules & validation
  │           └─ HTTP req/res handling
  └─ URL mapping
```

> **Rule:** Each layer only imports from the layer directly inside it. Never skip layers.

## Example Files

### 1. Config (Innermost)

````javascript
// filepath: c:\Users\Anand\Documents\Laravel\dating-app-api\src\config\database.js
const { Sequelize } = require('sequelize');
require('dotenv').config();

const sequelize = new Sequelize(
    process.env.DB_NAME || 'dating_app',
    process.env.DB_USER || 'root',
    process.env.DB_PASSWORD || '',
    {
        host: process.env.DB_HOST || 'localhost',
        dialect: 'mysql',
        logging: false,
        timezone: '+05:30',
        pool: { max: 5, min: 0, acquire: 30000, idle: 10000 }
    }
);

module.exports = sequelize;
````

### 2. Model Layer

````javascript
// filepath: c:\Users\Anand\Documents\Laravel\dating-app-api\src\models\userModel.js

        type: DataTypes.INTEGER,
        autoIncrement: true,
        primaryKey: true,
    },
    name: {
        type: DataTypes.STRING,
        allowNull: false,
    },
    email: {
        type: DataTypes.STRING,
        allowNull: false,
        unique: true,
    },

  │           │            │            │            │         └─ Pure Sequelize instance
  │           │            │            │            └─ Entity definitions (schema)
  │           │            │            └─ Raw DB queries (findAll, create, etc.)
  │           │            └─ Business rules & validation
  │           └─ HTTP req/res handling
  └─ URL mapping
```

> **Rule:** Each layer only imports from the layer directly inside it. Never skip layers.

## Example Files

### 1. Config (Innermost)

````javascript
// filepath: c:\Users\Anand\Documents\Laravel\dating-app-api\src\config\database.js
const { Sequelize } = require('sequelize');
require('dotenv').config();

const sequelize = new Sequelize(
    process.env.DB_NAME || 'dating_app',
    process.env.DB_USER || 'root',
    process.env.DB_PASSWORD || '',
    {
        host: process.env.DB_HOST || 'localhost',
        dialect: 'mysql',
        logging: false,
        timezone: '+05:30',
        pool: { max: 5, min: 0, acquire: 30000, idle: 10000 }
    }
);

module.exports = sequelize;
````

## Folder Structure

```
dating-app-api/
│
├── server.js                  # Entry point: starts Express server
├── syncDatabase.js            # DB creation & table sync utility
├── package.json
├── .env
│
└── src/
    │
    ├── config/
    │   └── database.js        # Sequelize instance (innermost layer - no dependencies)
    │
    ├── models/                # Domain/Entity layer
    │   ├── index.js           # Central hub: registers all models + associations
    │   ├── userModel.js       # User entity definition
    │   ├── profileModel.js    # Profile entity definition
    │   ├── matchModel.js      # Match entity definition
    │   └── messageModel.js    # Message entity definition
    │
    ├── repositories/          # Data Access layer (talks to models only)
    │   ├── userRepository.js  # CRUD operations for User
    │   ├── profileRepository.js
    │   ├── matchRepository.js
    │   └── messageRepository.js
    │
    ├── services/              # Business Logic layer (talks to repositories only)
    │   ├── authService.js     # Login, register, token logic
    │   ├── userService.js     # User-related business rules
    │   ├── profileService.js  # Profile business rules
    │   ├── matchService.js    # Matching algorithm & rules
    │   └── messageService.js  # Messaging business rules
    │
    ├── controllers/           # Interface/Adapter layer (talks to services only)
    │   ├── authController.js  # Handles auth HTTP requests
    │   ├── userController.js  # Handles user HTTP requests
    │   ├── profileController.js
    │   ├── matchController.js
    │   └── messageController.js
    │
    ├── routes/                # Route definitions (maps URLs to controllers)
    │   ├── index.js           # Central route hub
    │   ├── authRoutes.js
    │   ├── userRoutes.js
    │   ├── profileRoutes.js
    │   ├── matchRoutes.js
    │   └── messageRoutes.js
    │
    ├── middlewares/            # Cross-cutting concerns
    │   ├── authMiddleware.js  # JWT verification
    │   ├── errorHandler.js    # Global error handler
    │   └── validator.js       # Request validation
    │
    └── utils/                 # Shared helpers
        ├── apiResponse.js     # Standardized API responses
        ├── constants.js       # App-wide constants
        └── helpers.js         # Utility functions
```

dating-app-api/
│
├── server.js                  # Entry point: starts Express server
├── syncDatabase.js            # DB creation & table sync utility
├── package.json
├── .env
│
└── src/
    │
    ├── config/
    │   └── database.js        # Sequelize instance (innermost layer - no dependencies)
    │
    ├── models/                # Domain/Entity layer
    │   ├── index.js           # Central hub: registers all models + associations
    │   ├── userModel.js       # User entity definition
    │   ├── profileModel.js    # Profile entity definition
    │   ├── matchModel.js      # Match entity definition
    │   └── messageModel.js    # Message entity definition
    │
    ├── repositories/          # Data Access layer (talks to models only)
    │   ├── userRepository.js  # CRUD operations for User
    │   ├── profileRepository.js
    │   ├── matchRepository.js
    │   └── messageRepository.js
    │
    ├── services/              # Business Logic layer (talks to repositories only)
    │   ├── authService.js     # Login, register, token logic
    │   ├── userService.js     # User-related business rules
    │   ├── profileService.js  # Profile business rules
    │   ├── matchService.js    # Matching algorithm & rules
    │   └── messageService.js  # Messaging business rules
    │
    ├── controllers/           # Interface/Adapter layer (talks to services only)
    │   ├── authController.js  # Handles auth HTTP requests
    │   ├── userController.js  # Handles user HTTP requests
    │   ├── profileController.js
    │   ├── matchController.js
    │   └── messageController.js
    │
    ├── routes/                # Route definitions (maps URLs to controllers)
    │   ├── index.js           # Central route hub
    │   ├── authRoutes.js
    │   ├── userRoutes.js
    │   ├── profileRoutes.js
    │   ├── matchRoutes.js
    │   └── messageRoutes.js
    │
    ├── middlewares/            # Cross-cutting concerns
    │   ├── authMiddleware.js  # JWT verification
    │   ├── errorHandler.js    # Global error handler
    │   └── validator.js       # Request validation
    │
    └── utils/                 # Shared helpers
        ├── apiResponse.js     # Standardized API responses
        ├── constants.js       # App-wide constants
        └── helpers.js         # Utility functions
```

## Dependency Flow (Onion Layers)

```
Outermost ──────────────────────────────────── Innermost

Routes → Controllers → Services → Repositories → Models → Config/DB
  │           │            │            │            │         │
  │           │            │            │            │         └─ Pure Sequelize instance
  │           │            │            │            └─ Entity definitions (schema)
  │           │            │            └─ Raw DB queries (findAll, create, etc.)
  │           │            └─ Business rules & validation
  │           └─ HTTP req/res handling
  └─ URL mapping
```

> **Rule:** Each layer only imports from the layer directly inside it. Never skip layers.

## Example Files

### 1. Config (Innermost)

````javascript
// filepath: c:\Users\Anand\Documents\Laravel\dating-app-api\src\config\database.js
const { Sequelize } = require('sequelize');
require('dotenv').config();

const sequelize = new Sequelize(
    process.env.DB_NAME || 'dating_app',
    process.env.DB_USER || 'root',
    process.env.DB_PASSWORD || '',
    {
        host: process.env.DB_HOST || 'localhost',
        dialect: 'mysql',
        logging: false,
        timezone: '+05:30',
        pool: { max: 5, min: 0, acquire: 30000, idle: 10000 }
    }
);

module.exports = sequelize;
````

### 2. Model Layer

````javascript
// filepath: c:\Users\Anand\Documents\Laravel\dating-app-api\src\models\userModel.js
const { DataTypes } = require('sequelize');
const sequelize = require('../config/database');

const User = sequelize.define('User', {
    id: {
        type: DataTypes.INTEGER,
        autoIncrement: true,
        primaryKey: true,
    },
    name: {
        type: DataTypes.STRING,
        allowNull: false,
    },
    email: {
        type: DataTypes.STRING,
        allowNull: false,
        unique: true,
    },
    password: {
        type: DataTypes.STRING,
        allowNull: false,
    },
    phone: {
        type: DataTypes.STRING,
        allowNull: true,
    },
    gender
```

```
dating-app-api/
│
├── server.js                  # Entry point: starts Express server

├── package.json
├── .env
│
└── src/
    │
    ├── config/
    │   └── database.js        # Sequelize instance (innermost layer - no dependencies)
    │
    ├── models/                # Domain/Entity layer
    │   ├── index.js           # Central hub: registers all models + associations
    │   ├── userModel.js       # User entity definition
    │   ├── profileModel.js    # Profile entity definition
    │   ├── matchModel.js      # Match entity definition
    │   └── messageModel.js    # Message entity definition
    │
    ├── repositories/          # Data Access layer (talks to models only)
    │   ├── userRepository.js  # CRUD operations for User
    │   ├── profileRepository.js
    │   ├── matchRepository.js
    │   └── messageRepository.js
    │
    ├── services/              # Business Logic layer (talks to repositories only)
    │   ├── authService.js     # Login, register, token logic
    │   ├── userService.js     # User-related business rules
    │   ├── profileService.js  # Profile business rules
    │   ├── matchService.js    # Matching algorithm & rules
    │   └── messageService.js  # Messaging business rules
    │
    ├── controllers/           # Interface/Adapter layer (talks to services only)
    │   ├── authController.js  # Handles auth HTTP requests
    │   ├── userController.js  # Handles user HTTP requests
    │   ├── profileController.js
    │   ├── matchController.js
    │   └── messageController.js
    │
    ├── routes/                # Route definitions (maps URLs to controllers)
    │   ├── index.js           # Central route hub
    │   ├── authRoutes.js
    │   ├── userRoutes.js
    │   ├── profileRoutes.js
    │   ├── matchRoutes.js
    │   └── messageRoutes.js
    │
    ├── middlewares/            # Cross-cutting concerns
    │   ├── authMiddleware.js  # JWT verification
    │   ├── errorHandler.js    # Global error handler
    │   └── validator.js       # Request validation
    │
    └── utils/                 # Shared helpers
        ├── apiResponse.js     # Standardized API responses
        ├── constants.js       # App-wide constants
        └── helpers.js         # Utility functions
```

    ├── middlewares/            # Cross-cutting concerns
    │   ├── authMiddleware.js  # JWT verification
    │   ├── errorHandler.js    # Global error handler
    │   └── validator.js       # Request validation
    │
    └── utils/                 # Shared helpers
        ├── apiResponse.js     # Standardized API responses
        ├── constants.js       # App-wide constants
        └── helpers.js         # Utility functions
```

## Dependency Flow (Onion Layers)

```
Outermost ──────────────────────────────────── Innermost

Routes → Controllers → Services → Repositories → Models → Config/DB
  │           │            │            │            │         │
  │           │            │            │            │         └─ Pure Sequelize instance
  │           │            │            │            └─ Entity definitions (schema)
  │           │            │            └─ Raw DB queries (findAll, create, etc.)
  │           │            └─ Business rules & validation
  │           └─ HTTP req/res handling
  └─ URL mapping
```

> **Rule:** Each layer only imports from the layer directly inside it. Never skip layers.

## Example Files

### 1. Config (Innermost)

````javascript

require('dotenv').config();

const sequelize = new Sequelize(
    process.env.DB_NAME || 'dating_app',
    process.env.DB_USER || 'root',
    process.env.DB_PASSWORD || '',
    {
        host: process.env.DB_HOST || 'localhost',
        dialect: 'mysql',
        logging: false,
        timezone: '+05:30',
        pool: { max: 5, min: 0, acquire: 30000, idle: 10000 }
    }
);

module.exports = sequelize;

## Dependency Flow (Onion Layers)

```
Outermost ──────────────────────────────────── Innermost

Routes → Controllers → Services → Repositories → Models → Config/DB
  │           │            │            │            │         │
  │           │            │            │            │         └─ Pure Sequelize instance
  │           │            │            │            └─ Entity definitions (schema)

  │           └─ HTTP req/res handling
  └─ URL mapping
```

> **Rule:** Each layer only imports from the layer directly inside it. Never skip layers.

    │   ├── authService.js     # Login, register, token logic
    │   ├── userService.js     # User-related business rules
    │   ├── profileService.js  # Profile business rules
    │   ├── matchService.js    # Matching algorithm & rules
    │   └── messageService.js  # Messaging business rules
    │
    ├── controllers/           # Interface/Adapter layer (talks to services only)
    │   ├── authController.js  # Handles auth HTTP requests
    │   ├── userController.js  # Handles user HTTP requests
    │   ├── profileController.js
    │   ├── matchController.js
    │   └── messageController.js
    │
    ├── routes/                # Route definitions (maps URLs to controllers)
    │   ├── index.js           # Central route hub
    │   ├── authRoutes.js
    │   ├── userRoutes.js
    │   ├── profileRoutes.js
    │   ├── matchRoutes.js
    │   └── messageRoutes.js
    │
    ├── middlewares/            # Cross-cutting concerns
    │   ├── authMiddleware.js  # JWT verification
    │   ├── errorHandler.js    # Global error handler
    │   └── validator.js       # Request validation
    │
    └── utils/                 # Shared helpers
        ├── apiResponse.js     # Standardized API responses
        ├── constants.js       # App-wide constants
        └── helpers.js         # Utility functions
```

## Dependency Flow (Onion Layers)

```
Outermost ──────────────────────────────────── Innermost

Routes → Controllers → Services → Repositories → Models → Config/DB
  │           │            │            │            │         │
  │           │            │            │            │         └─ Pure Sequelize instance
  │           │            │            │            └─ Entity definitions (schema)
  │           │            │            └─ Raw DB queries (findAll, create, etc.)
  │           │            └─ Business rules & validation
  │           └─ HTTP req/res handling
  └─ URL mapping
```

> **Rule:** Each layer only imports from the layer directly inside it. Never skip layers.

## Example Files

### 1. Config (Innermost)

````javascript
// filepath: c:\Users\Anand\Documents\Laravel\dating-app-api\src\config\database.js
const { Sequelize } = require('sequelize');
require('dotenv').config();

const sequelize = new Sequelize(
    process.env.DB_NAME || 'dating_app',
    process.env.DB_USER || 'root',
    process.env.DB_PASSWORD || '',
    {
        host: process.env.DB_HOST || 'localhost',
        dialect: 'mysql',
        logging: false,
        timezone: '+05:30',
        pool: { max: 5, min: 0, acquire: 30000, idle: 10000 }
    }
);

module.exports = sequelize;
````

### 2. Model Layer

````javascript
// filepath: c:\Users\Anand\Documents\Laravel\dating-app-api\src\models\userModel.js
const { DataTypes } = require('sequelize');
const sequelize = require('../config/database');

const User = sequelize.define('User', {
    id: {
        type: DataTypes.INTEGER,
        autoIncrement: true,
        primaryKey: true,
    },
    name: {
        type: DataTypes.STRING,
        allowNull: false,
    },
    email: {
        type: DataTypes.STRING,
        allowNull: false,
        unique: true,
    },
    password: {
        type: DataTypes.STRING,
        allowNull: false,
    },
    phone: {
        type: DataTypes.STRING,
        allowNull: true,
    },
    gender
```

  │           │            │            │            └─ Entity definitions (schema)
  │           │            │            └─ Raw DB queries (findAll, create, etc.)
  │           │            └─ Business rules & validation
  │           └─ HTTP req/res handling
  └─ URL mapping
```

> **Rule:** Each layer only imports from the layer directly inside it. Never skip layers.

## Example Files

### 1. Config (Innermost)

````javascript

    process.env.DB_NAME || 'dating_app',

    process.env.DB_PASSWORD || '',
    {

        dialect: 'mysql',
        logging: false,
        timezone: '+05:30',
        pool: { max: 5, min: 0, acquire: 30000, idle: 10000 }
    }
);

module.exports = sequelize;
````

Here's a complete onion architecture for your dating app API:

## Folder Structure

```
dating-app-api/
│
├── server.js                  # Entry point: starts Express server
├── syncDatabase.js            # DB creation & table sync utility
├── package.json
├── .env
│
└── src/
    │
    ├── config/
    │   └── database.js        # Sequelize instance (innermost layer - no dependencies)
    │
    ├── models/                # Domain/Entity layer
    │   ├── index.js           # Central hub: registers all models + associations
    │   ├── userModel.js       # User entity definition
    │   ├── profileModel.js    # Profile entity definition
    │   ├── matchModel.js      # Match entity definition
    │   └── messageModel.js    # Message entity definition
    │
    ├── repositories/          # Data Access layer (talks to models only)
    │   ├── userRepository.js  # CRUD operations for User
    │   ├── profileRepository.js
    │   ├── matchRepository.js
    │   └── messageRepository.js
    │
    ├── services/              # Business Logic layer (talks to repositories only)
    │   ├── authService.js     # Login, register, token logic
    │   ├── userService.js     # User-related business rules
    │   ├── profileService.js  # Profile business rules
    │   ├── matchService.js    # Matching algorithm & rules
    │   └── messageService.js  # Messaging business rules
    │
    ├── controllers/           # Interface/Adapter layer (talks to services only)
    │   ├── authController.js  # Handles auth HTTP requests
    │   ├── userController.js  # Handles user HTTP requests
    │   ├── profileController.js
    │   ├── matchController.js
    │   └── messageController.js
    │
    ├── routes/                # Route definitions (maps URLs to controllers)
    │   ├── index.js           # Central route hub
    │   ├── authRoutes.js
    │   ├── userRoutes.js
    │   ├── profileRoutes.js
    │   ├── matchRoutes.js
    │   └── messageRoutes.js
    │
    ├── middlewares/            # Cross-cutting concerns
    │   ├── authMiddleware.js  # JWT verification
    │   ├── errorHandler.js    # Global error handler
    │   └── validator.js       # Request validation
    │
    └── utils/                 # Shared helpers
        ├── apiResponse.js     # Standardized API responses
        ├── constants.js       # App-wide constants
        └── helpers.js         # Utility functions
```

Here's a complete onion architecture for your dating app API:

## Folder Structure

```
dating-app-api/
│
├── server.js                  # Entry point: starts Express server
├── syncDatabase.js            # DB creation & table sync utility
├── package.json
├── .env
│
└── src/
    │
    ├── config/
    │   └── database.js        # Sequelize instance (innermost layer - no dependencies)
    │
    ├── models/                # Domain/Entity layer
    │   ├── index.js           # Central hub: registers all models + associations
    │   ├── userModel.js       # User entity definition
    │   ├── profileModel.js    # Profile entity definition
    │   ├── matchModel.js      # Match entity definition
    │   └── messageModel.js    # Message entity definition
    │
    ├── repositories/          # Data Access layer (talks to models only)
    │   ├── userRepository.js  # CRUD operations for User
    │   ├── profileRepository.js
    │   ├── matchRepository.js
    │   └── messageRepository.js
    │
    ├── services/              # Business Logic layer (talks to repositories only)
    │   ├── authService.js     # Login, register, token logic
    │   ├── userService.js     # User-related business rules
    │   ├── profileService.js  # Profile business rules
    │   ├── matchService.js    # Matching algorithm & rules
    │   └── messageService.js  # Messaging business rules
    │
    ├── controllers/           # Interface/Adapter layer (talks to services only)
    │   ├── authController.js  # Handles auth HTTP requests
    │   ├── userController.js  # Handles user HTTP requests
    │   ├── profileController.js
    │   ├── matchController.js
    │   └── messageController.js
    │
    ├── routes/                # Route definitions (maps URLs to controllers)
    │   ├── index.js           # Central route hub
    │   ├── authRoutes.js
    │   ├── userRoutes.js
    │   ├── profileRoutes.js
    │   ├── matchRoutes.js
    │   └── messageRoutes.js
    │
    ├── middlewares/            # Cross-cutting concerns
    │   ├── authMiddleware.js  # JWT verification
    │   ├── errorHandler.js    # Global error handler
    │   └── validator.js       # Request validation
    │
    └── utils/                 # Shared helpers
        ├── apiResponse.js     # Standardized API responses
        ├── constants.js       # App-wide constants
        └── helpers.js         # Utility functions
```

## Dependency Flow (Onion Layers)

```
Outermost ──────────────────────────────────── Innermost

Routes → Controllers → Services → Repositories → Models → Config/DB
  │           │            │            │            │         │
  │           │            │            │            │         └─ Pure Sequelize instance
  │           │            │            │            └─ Entity definitions (schema)
  │           │            │            └─ Raw DB queries (findAll, create, etc.)
  │           │            └─ Business rules & validation
  │           └─ HTTP req/res handling
  └─ URL mapping
```

> **Rule:** Each layer only imports from the layer directly inside it. Never skip layers.

## Example Files

### 1. Config (Innermost)

````javascript
// filepath: c:\Users\Anand\Documents\Laravel\dating-app-api\src\config\database.js
const { Sequelize } = require('sequelize');
require('dotenv').config();

const sequelize = new Sequelize(
    process.env.DB_NAME || 'dating_app',
    process.env.DB_USER || 'root',
    process.env.DB_PASSWORD || '',
    {
        host: process.env.DB_HOST || 'localhost',
        dialect: 'mysql',
        logging: false,
        timezone: '+05:30',
        pool: { max: 5, min: 0, acquire: 30000, idle: 10000 }
    }
);

module.exports = sequelize;
````

### 2. Model Layer

````javascript
// filepath: c:\Users\Anand\Documents\Laravel\dating-app-api\src\models\userModel.js
const { DataTypes } = require('sequelize');
const sequelize = require('../config/database');

const User = sequelize.define('User', {
    id: {
        type: DataTypes.INTEGER,
        autoIncrement: true,
        primaryKey: true,
    },
    name: {
        type: DataTypes.STRING,
        allowNull: false,
    },
    email: {
        type: DataTypes.STRING,
        allowNull: false,
        unique: true,
    },
    password: {
        type: DataTypes.STRING,
        allowNull: false,
    },
    phone
```

    ├── controllers/           # Interface/Adapter layer (talks to services only)
    │   ├── authController.js  # Handles auth HTTP requests
    │   ├── userController.js  # Handles user HTTP requests
    │   ├── profileController.js
    │   ├── matchController.js
    │   └── messageController.js
    │
    ├── routes/                # Route definitions (maps URLs to controllers)
    │   ├── index.js           # Central route hub
    │   ├── authRoutes.js
    │   ├── userRoutes.js
    │   ├── profileRoutes.js
    │   ├── matchRoutes.js
    │   └── messageRoutes.js
    │
    ├── middlewares/            # Cross-cutting concerns
    │   ├── authMiddleware.js  # JWT verification
    │   ├── errorHandler.js    # Global error handler
    │   └── validator.js       # Request validation
    │
    └── utils/                 # Shared helpers
        ├── apiResponse.js     # Standardized API responses
        ├── constants.js       # App-wide constants
        └── helpers.js         # Utility functions
```

## Dependency Flow (Onion Layers)

```
Outermost ──────────────────────────────────── Innermost

Routes → Controllers → Services → Repositories → Models → Config/DB
  │           │            │            │            │         │
  │           │            │            │            │         └─ Pure Sequelize instance
  │           │            │            │            └─ Entity definitions (schema)
  │           │            │            └─ Raw DB queries (findAll, create, etc.)
  │           │            └─ Business rules & validation
  │           └─ HTTP req/res handling
  └─ URL mapping
```

> **Rule:** Each layer only imports from the layer directly inside it. Never skip layers.

## Example Files

### 1. Config (Innermost)

````javascript
// filepath: c:\Users\Anand\Documents\Laravel\dating-app-api\src\config\database.js
const { Sequelize } = require('sequelize');
require('dotenv').config();

const sequelize = new Sequelize(
    process.env.DB_NAME || 'dating_app',
    process.env.DB_USER || 'root',
    process.env.DB_PASSWORD || '',
    {
        host: process.env.DB_HOST || 'localhost',
        dialect: 'mysql',
        logging: false,
        timezone: '+05:30',
        pool: { max: 5, min: 0, acquire: 30000, idle: 10000 }
    }
);

module.exports = sequelize;
````

### 2. Model Layer

````javascript
// filepath: c:\Users\Anand\Documents\Laravel\dating-app-api\src\models\userModel.js
const { DataTypes } = require('sequelize');
const sequelize = require('../config/database');

const User = sequelize.define('User', {
    id: {
        type: DataTypes.INTEGER,
        autoIncrement: true,
        primaryKey: true,
    },
    name: {
        type: DataTypes.STRING,
        allowNull: false,
    },
    email: {
        type: DataTypes.STRING,
        allowNull: false,
        unique: true,
    },
    password: {
        type: DataTypes.STRING,
        allowNull: false,
    },
    phone: {
        type: DataTypes.STRING,
        allowNull: true,
    },
    gender
```

    │   ├── matchService.js    # Matching algorithm & rules

    │

        allowNull: false,
    },

```


Here's a complete onion architecture for your dating app API:

## Folder Structure

```
dating-app-api/
│
├── server.js                  # Entry point: starts Express server
├── syncDatabase.js            # DB creation & table sync utility
├── package.json
├── .env
│
└── src/
    │
    ├── config/
    │   └── database.js        # Sequelize instance (innermost layer - no dependencies)
    │
    ├── models/                # Domain/Entity layer
    │   ├── index.js           # Central hub: registers all models + associations
    │   ├── userModel.js       # User entity definition
    │   ├── profileModel.js    # Profile entity definition
    │   ├── matchModel.js      # Match entity definition
    │   └── messageModel.js    # Message entity definition
    │
    ├── repositories/          # Data Access layer (talks to models only)
    │   ├── userRepository.js  # CRUD operations for User
    │   ├── profileRepository.js
    │   ├── matchRepository.js
    │   └── messageRepository.js
    │
    ├── services/              # Business Logic layer (talks to repositories only)
    │   ├── authService.js     # Login, register, token logic
    │   ├── userService.js     # User-related business rules
    │   ├── profileService.js  # Profile business rules
    │   ├── matchService.js    # Matching algorithm & rules
    │   └── messageService.js  # Messaging business rules
    │
    ├── controllers/           # Interface/Adapter layer (talks to services only)
    │   ├── authController.js  # Handles auth HTTP requests
    │   ├── userController.js  # Handles user HTTP requests
    │   ├── profileController.js
    │   ├── matchController.js
    │   └── messageController.js
    │
    ├── routes/                # Route definitions (maps URLs to controllers)
    │   ├── index.js           # Central route hub
    │   ├── authRoutes.js
    │   ├── userRoutes.js
    │   ├── profileRoutes.js
    │   ├── matchRoutes.js
    │   └── messageRoutes.js
    │
    ├── middlewares/            # Cross-cutting concerns
    │   ├── authMiddleware.js  # JWT verification
    │   ├── errorHandler.js    # Global error handler
    │   └── validator.js       # Request validation
    │
    └── utils/                 # Shared helpers
        ├── apiResponse.js     # Standardized API responses
        ├── constants.js       # App-wide constants
        └── helpers.js         # Utility functions
```

## Dependency Flow (Onion Layers)

```
Outermost ──────────────────────────────────── Innermost

Routes → Controllers → Services → Repositories → Models → Config/DB
  │           │            │            │            │         │
  │           │            │            │            │         └─ Pure Sequelize instance
  │           │            │            │            └─ Entity definitions (schema)
  │           │            │            └─ Raw DB queries (findAll, create, etc.)
  │           │            └─ Business rules & validation
  │           └─ HTTP req/res handling
  └─ URL mapping
```

> **Rule:** Each layer only imports from the layer directly inside it. Never skip layers.

## Example Files

### 1. Config (Innermost)

````javascript
// filepath: c:\Users\Anand\Documents\Laravel\dating-app-api\src\config\database.js
const { Sequelize } = require('sequelize');
require('dotenv').config();

const sequelize = new Sequelize(
    process.env.DB_NAME || 'dating_app',
    process.env.DB_USER || 'root',
    process.env.DB_PASSWORD || '',
    {
        host: process.env.DB_HOST || 'localhost',
        dialect: 'mysql',
        logging: false,
        timezone: '+05:30',
        pool: { max: 5, min: 0, acquire: 30000, idle: 10000 }
    }
);

module.exports = sequelize;
````

### 2. Model Layer

````javascript
// filepath: c:\Users\Anand\Documents\Laravel\dating-app-api\src\models\userModel.js
const { DataTypes } = require('sequelize');
const sequelize = require('../config/database');

const User = sequelize.define('User', {
    id: {
        type: DataTypes.INTEGER,
        autoIncrement: true,
        primaryKey: true,
    },
    name: {
        type: DataTypes.STRING,
        allowNull: false,
    },
    email: {
        type: DataTypes.STRING,
        allowNull: false,
        unique: true,
    },
    password: {
        type: DataTypes.STRING,
        allowNull: false,
    },
    phone
```

    │   ├── userController.js  # Handles user HTTP requests
    │   ├── profileController.js
    │   ├── matchController.js
    │   └── messageController.js
    │
    ├── routes/                # Route definitions (maps URLs to controllers)
    │   ├── index.js           # Central route hub
    │   ├── authRoutes.js
    │   ├── userRoutes.js
    │   ├── profileRoutes.js
    │   ├── matchRoutes.js
    │   └── messageRoutes.js
    │
    ├── middlewares/            # Cross-cutting concerns
    │   ├── authMiddleware.js  # JWT verification
    │   ├── errorHandler.js    # Global error handler
    │   └── validator.js       # Request validation
    │
    └── utils/                 # Shared helpers
        ├── apiResponse.js     # Standardized API responses
        ├── constants.js       # App-wide constants
        └── helpers.js         # Utility functions
```

## Dependency Flow (Onion Layers)

```
Outermost ──────────────────────────────────── Innermost

Routes → Controllers → Services → Repositories → Models → Config/DB
  │           │            │            │            │         │
  │           │            │            │            │         └─ Pure Sequelize instance
  │           │            │            │            └─ Entity definitions (schema)
  │           │            │            └─ Raw DB queries (findAll, create, etc.)
  │           │            └─ Business rules & validation
  │           └─ HTTP req/res handling
  └─ URL mapping
```

> **Rule:** Each layer only imports from the layer directly inside it. Never skip layers.

## Example Files

### 1. Config (Innermost)

````javascript
// filepath: c:\Users\Anand\Documents\Laravel\dating-app-api\src\config\database.js
const { Sequelize } = require('sequelize');
require('dotenv').config();

const sequelize = new Sequelize(
    process.env.DB_NAME || 'dating_app',
    process.env.DB_USER || 'root',
    process.env.DB_PASSWORD || '',
    {
        host: process.env.DB_HOST || 'localhost',
        dialect: 'mysql',
        logging: false,
        timezone: '+05:30',
        pool: { max: 5, min: 0, acquire: 30000, idle: 10000 }
    }
);

module.exports = sequelize;
````

  │           │            │            │            │         └─ Pure Sequelize instance
  │           │            │            │            └─ Entity definitions (schema)
  │           │            │            └─ Raw DB queries (findAll, create, etc.)
  │           │            └─ Business rules & validation
  │           └─ HTTP req/res handling
  └─ URL mapping
```

> **Rule:** Each layer only imports from the layer directly inside it. Never skip layers.

## Example Files

### 1. Config (Innermost)

````javascript
// filepath: c:\Users\Anand\Documents\Laravel\dating-app-api\src\config\database.js
const { Sequelize } = require('sequelize');
require('dotenv').config();

const sequelize = new Sequelize(
    process.env.DB_NAME || 'dating_app',
    process.env.DB_USER || 'root',
    process.env.DB_PASSWORD || '',
    {
        host: process.env.DB_HOST || 'localhost',
        dialect: 'mysql',
        logging: false,
        timezone: '+05:30',
        pool: { max: 5, min: 0, acquire: 30000, idle: 10000 }
    }
);

module.exports = sequelize;
````

### 2. Model Layer

````javascript
// filepath: c:\Users\Anand\Documents\Laravel\dating-app-api\src\models\userModel.js
const { DataTypes } = require('sequelize');
const sequelize = require('../config/database');

const User = sequelize.define('User', {
    id: {
        type: DataTypes.INTEGER,
        autoIncrement: true,
        primaryKey: true,
    },
    name: {
        type: DataTypes.STRING,
        allowNull: false,
    },
    email: {
        type: DataTypes.STRING,
        allowNull: false,
        unique: true,
    },
    password: {
        type: DataTypes.STRING,
        allowNull: false,
    },
    phone: {
        type: DataTypes.STRING,
        allowNull: true,
    },
    gender: {
        type: DataTypes
```

Here's a complete onion architecture for your dating app API:

## Folder Structure

```
dating-app-api/
│
├── server.js                  # Entry point: starts Express server
├── syncDatabase.js            # DB creation & table sync utility
├── package.json
├── .env
│
└── src/
    │
    ├── config/
    │   └── database.js        # Sequelize instance (innermost layer - no dependencies)
    │
    ├── models/                # Domain/Entity layer
    │   ├── index.js           # Central hub: registers all models + associations
    │   ├── userModel.js       # User entity definition
    │   ├── profileModel.js    # Profile entity definition
    │   ├── matchModel.js      # Match entity definition
    │   └── messageModel.js    # Message entity definition
    │
    ├── repositories/          # Data Access layer (talks to models only)
    │   ├── userRepository.js  # CRUD operations for User
    │   ├── profileRepository.js
    │   ├── matchRepository.js
    │   └── messageRepository.js
    │
    ├── services/              # Business Logic layer (talks to repositories only)
    │   ├── authService.js     # Login, register, token logic
    │   ├── userService.js     # User-related business rules
    │   ├── profileService.js  # Profile business rules
    │   ├── matchService.js    # Matching algorithm & rules
    │   └── messageService.js  # Messaging business rules
    │
    ├── controllers/           # Interface/Adapter layer (talks to services only)
    │   ├── authController.js  # Handles auth HTTP requests
    │   ├── userController.js  # Handles user HTTP requests
    │   ├── profileController.js
    │   ├── matchController.js
    │   └── messageController.js
    │
    ├── routes/                # Route definitions (maps URLs to controllers)
    │   ├── index.js           # Central route hub
    │   ├── authRoutes.js
    │   ├── userRoutes.js
    │   ├── profileRoutes.js
    │   ├── matchRoutes.js
    │   └── messageRoutes.js
    │
    ├── middlewares/            # Cross-cutting concerns
    │   ├── authMiddleware.js  # JWT verification
    │   ├── errorHandler.js    # Global error handler
    │   └── validator.js       # Request validation
    │
    └── utils/                 # Shared helpers
        ├── apiResponse.js     # Standardized API responses
        ├── constants.js       # App-wide constants
        └── helpers.js         # Utility functions
```

Here's a complete onion architecture for your dating app API:

## Folder Structure

```
dating-app-api/
│
├── server.js                  # Entry point: starts Express server
├── syncDatabase.js            # DB creation & table sync utility
├── package.json
├── .env
│
└── src/
    │
    ├── config/
    │   └── database.js        # Sequelize instance (innermost layer - no dependencies)
    │
    ├── models/                # Domain/Entity layer
    │   ├── index.js           # Central hub: registers all models + associations
    │   ├── userModel.js       # User entity definition
    │   ├── profileModel.js    # Profile entity definition
    │   ├── matchModel.js      # Match entity definition
    │   └── messageModel.js    # Message entity definition
    │
    ├── repositories/          # Data Access layer (talks to models only)
    │   ├── userRepository.js  # CRUD operations for User
    │   ├── profileRepository.js
    │   ├── matchRepository.js
    │   └── messageRepository.js
    │
    ├── services/              # Business Logic layer (talks to repositories only)
    │   ├── authService.js     # Login, register, token logic
    │   ├── userService.js     # User-related business rules
    │   ├── profileService.js  # Profile business rules
    │   ├── matchService.js    # Matching algorithm & rules
    │   └── messageService.js  # Messaging business rules
    │
    ├── controllers/           # Interface/Adapter layer (talks to services only)
    │   ├── authController.js  # Handles auth HTTP requests
    │   ├── userController.js  # Handles user HTTP requests
    │   ├── profileController.js
    │   ├── matchController.js
    │   └── messageController.js
    │
    ├── routes/                # Route definitions (maps URLs to controllers)
    │   ├── index.js           # Central route hub
    │   ├── authRoutes.js
    │   ├── userRoutes.js
    │   ├── profileRoutes.js
    │   ├── matchRoutes.js
    │   └── messageRoutes.js
    │
    ├── middlewares/            # Cross-cutting concerns
    │   ├── authMiddleware.js  # JWT verification
    │   ├── errorHandler.js    # Global error handler
    │   └── validator.js       # Request validation
    │
    └── utils/                 # Shared helpers
        ├── apiResponse.js     # Standardized API responses
        ├── constants.js       # App-wide constants
        └── helpers.js         # Utility functions
```

## Dependency Flow (Onion Layers)

```
Outermost ──────────────────────────────────── Innermost

Routes → Controllers → Services → Repositories → Models → Config/DB
  │           │            │            │            │         │
  │           │            │            │            │         └─ Pure Sequelize instance
  │           │            │            │            └─ Entity definitions (schema)
  │           │            │            └─ Raw DB queries (findAll, create, etc.)
  │           │            └─ Business rules & validation
  │           └─ HTTP req/res handling
  └─ URL mapping
```

> **Rule:** Each layer only imports from the layer directly inside it. Never skip layers.

## Example Files

### 1. Config (Innermost)

````javascript
// filepath: c:\Users\Anand\Documents\Laravel\dating-app-api\src\config\database.js
const { Sequelize } = require('sequelize');
require('dotenv').config();

const sequelize = new Sequelize(
    process.env.DB_NAME || 'dating_app',
    process.env.DB_USER || 'root',
    process.env.DB_PASSWORD || '',
    {
        host: process.env.DB_HOST || 'localhost',
        dialect: 'mysql',
        logging: false,
        timezone: '+05:30',
        pool: { max: 5, min: 0, acquire: 30000, idle: 10000 }
    }
);

module.exports = sequelize;
````
