### 4. Service Layer (Business Logic)

````javascript
// filepath: c:\Users\Anand\Documents\Laravel\dating-app-api\src\services\userService.js
const userRepository = require('../repositories/userRepository');
const bcrypt = require('bcryptjs');

class UserService {
    async getAllUsers() {
        const users = await userRepository.findAll();
        // Strip passwords before returning
        return users.map(u => {
            const { password, ...safe } = u.toJSON();
            return safe;
        });
    }

    async getUserById(id) {
        const user = await userRepository.findById(id);
        if (!user) throw new Error('User not found');
        const { password, ...safe } = user.toJSON();
        return safe;
    }

    async createUser(data) {
        // Business rule: check if email already exists
        const existing = await userRepository.findByEmail(data.email);
        if (existing) throw new Error('Email already registered');

        // Business rule: hash password
        const salt = await bcrypt.genSalt(10);
        data.password = await bcrypt.hash(data.password, salt);

        const user = await userRepository.create(data);
        const { password, ...safe } = user.toJSON();
        return safe;
    }

    async updateUser(id, data) {
        // Business rule: don't allow email change to existing email
        if (data.email) {
            const existing = await userRepository.findByEmail(data.email);
            if (existing && existing.id !== id) throw new Error('Email already in use');
        }

        const user = await userRepository.update(id, data);
        if (!user) throw new Error('User not found');
        const { password, ...safe } = user.toJSON();
        return safe;
    }

    async deleteUser(id) {
        const result = await userRepository.delete(id);
        if (!result) throw new Error('User not found');
        return { message: 'User deleted successfully' };
    }
}

module.exports = new UserService();
````

            return apiResponse.success(res, 'User retrieved successfully', user);
        } catch (error) {

    }

    async create(req, res) {

        } catch (error) {

        }
    }

    async update(req, res) {
        try {

            return apiResponse.success(res, 'User updated successfully', user);
        } catch (error) {
            return apiResponse.error(res, error.message, 400);
        }
    }

    async delete(req, res) {
        try {
            const result = await userService.deleteUser(req.params.id);
            return apiResponse.success(res, result.message);
        } catch (error) {

}

// filepath: c:\Users\Anand\Documents\Laravel\dating-app-api\src\services\userService.js
const userRepository = require('../repositories/userRepository');
const bcrypt = require('bcryptjs');

class UserService {
    async getAllUsers() {
        const users = await userRepository.findAll();
        // Strip passwords before returning
        return users.map(u => {
            const { password, ...safe } = u.toJSON();
            return safe;
        });
    }

    async getUserById(id) {
        const user = await userRepository.findById(id);
        if (!user) throw new Error('User not found');
        const { password, ...safe } = user.toJSON();
        return safe;
    }

    async createUser(data) {
        // Business rule: check if email already exists
        const existing = await userRepository.findByEmail(data.email);
        if (existing) throw new Error('Email already registered');

        // Business rule: hash password
        const salt = await bcrypt.genSalt(10);
        data.password = await bcrypt.hash(data.password, salt);

        const user = await userRepository.create(data);
        const { password, ...safe } = user.toJSON();
        return safe;
    }

    async updateUser(id, data) {
        // Business rule: don't allow email change to existing email
        if (data.email) {
            const existing = await userRepository.findByEmail(data.email);
            if (existing && existing.id !== id) throw new Error('Email already in use');
        }

        const user = await userRepository.update(id, data);
        if (!user) throw new Error('User not found');
        const { password, ...safe } = user.toJSON();
        return safe;
    }

    async deleteUser(id) {
        const result = await userRepository.delete(id);
        if (!result) throw new Error('User not found');
        return { message: 'User deleted successfully' };
    }
}

module.exports = new UserService();
````

### 5. Controller Layer (HTTP Interface)

````javascript
// filepath: c:\Users\Anand\Documents\Laravel\dating-app-api\src\controllers\userController.js
const userService = require('../services/userService');
const apiResponse = require('../utils/apiResponse');

class UserController {
    async getAll(req, res) {
        try {
            const users = await userService.getAllUsers();
            return apiResponse.success(res, 'Users retrieved successfully', users);
        } catch (error) {
            return apiResponse.error(res, error.message);
        }
    }

    async getById(req, res) {
        try {
            const user = await userService.getUserById(req.params.id);
            return apiResponse.success(res, 'User retrieved successfully', user);
        } catch (error) {
            return apiResponse.error(res, error.message, 404);
        }
    }

    async create(req, res) {
        try {
            const user = await userService.createUser(req.body);
            return apiResponse.success(res, 'User created successfully', user, 201);
        } catch (error) {
            return apiResponse.error(res, error.message, 400);
        }
    }

    async update(req, res) {
        try {
            const user = await userService.updateUser(req.params.id, req.body);
            return apiResponse.success(res, 'User updated successfully', user);
        } catch (error) {
            return apiResponse.error(res, error.message, 400);
        }
    }

    async delete(req, res) {
        try {
            const result = await userService.deleteUser(req.params.id);
            return apiResponse.success(res, result.message);
        } catch (error) {
            return apiResponse.error(res, error.message, 400);
        }
    }
}

module.exports = new UserController();
````

### 6. Routes Layer (Outermost)

````javascript
// filepath: c:\Users\Anand\Documents\Laravel\dating-app-api\src\routes\userRoutes.js
const express = require('express');
const router = express.Router();
const userController = require('../controllers/userController');

router.get('/', userController.getAll);
router.get('/:id', userController.getById);
router.post('/', userController.create);
router.put('/:id', userController.update);
router.delete('/:id', userController.delete);

module.exports = router;
````

```


Here's a complete onion architecture for your dating app API:

## Folder Structure

```
dating-app-api/
│
├── server.js                  # Entry point: starts Express server
├── syncDatabase.js            # DB creation & table sync utility
├── package.json
├── .env
│
└── src/
    │
    ├── config/
    │   └── database.js        # Sequelize instance (innermost layer - no dependencies)
    │
    ├── models/                # Domain/Entity layer
    │   ├── index.js           # Central hub: registers all models + associations
    │   ├── userModel.js       # User entity definition
    │   ├── profileModel.js    # Profile entity definition
    │   ├── matchModel.js      # Match entity definition
    │   └── messageModel.js    # Message entity definition
    │
    ├── repositories/          # Data Access layer (talks to models only)
    │   ├── userRepository.js  # CRUD operations for User
    │   ├── profileRepository.js
    │   ├── matchRepository.js
    │   └── messageRepository.js
    │
    ├── services/              # Business Logic layer (talks to repositories only)
    │   ├── authService.js     # Login, register, token logic
    │   ├── userService.js     # User-related business rules
    │   ├── profileService.js  # Profile business rules
    │   ├── matchService.js    # Matching algorithm & rules
    │   └── messageService.js  # Messaging business rules
    │
    ├── controllers/           # Interface/Adapter layer (talks to services only)
    │   ├── authController.js  # Handles auth HTTP requests
    │   ├── userController.js  # Handles user HTTP requests
    │   ├── profileController.js
    │   ├── matchController.js
    │   └── messageController.js
    │
    ├── routes/                # Route definitions (maps URLs to controllers)
    │   ├── index.js           # Central route hub
    │   ├── authRoutes.js
    │   ├── userRoutes.js
    │   ├── profileRoutes.js
    │   ├── matchRoutes.js
    │   └── messageRoutes.js
    │
    ├── middlewares/            # Cross-cutting concerns
    │   ├── authMiddleware.js  # JWT verification
    │   ├── errorHandler.js    # Global error handler
    │   └── validator.js       # Request validation
    │
    └── utils/                 # Shared helpers
        ├── apiResponse.js     # Standardized API responses
        ├── constants.js       # App-wide constants
        └── helpers.js         # Utility functions
```

## Dependency Flow (Onion Layers)

```
Outermost ──────────────────────────────────── Innermost

Routes → Controllers → Services → Repositories → Models → Config/DB
  │           │            │            │            │         │
  │           │            │            │            │         └─ Pure Sequelize instance
  │           │            │            │            └─ Entity definitions (schema)
  │           │            │            └─ Raw DB queries (findAll, create, etc.)
  │           │            └─ Business rules & validation
  │           └─ HTTP req/res handling
  └─ URL mapping
```

> **Rule:** Each layer only imports from the layer directly inside it. Never skip layers.

## Example Files

### 1. Config (Innermost)

````javascript
// filepath: c:\Users\Anand\Documents\Laravel\dating-app-api\src\config\database.js
const { Sequelize } = require('sequelize');
require('dotenv').config();

const sequelize = new Sequelize(
    process.env.DB_NAME || 'dating_app',
    process.env.DB_USER || 'root',
    process.env.DB_PASSWORD || '',
    {
        host: process.env.DB_HOST || 'localhost',
        dialect: 'mysql',
        logging: false,
        timezone: '+05:30',
        pool: { max: 5, min: 0, acquire: 30000, idle: 10000 }
    }
);

module.exports = sequelize;
````

### 2. Model Layer

````javascript
// filepath: c:\Users\Anand\Documents\Laravel\dating-app-api\src\models\userModel.js
const { DataTypes } = require('sequelize');
const sequelize = require('../config/database');

const User = sequelize.define('User', {
    id: {
        type: DataTypes.INTEGER,
        autoIncrement: true,
        primaryKey: true,
    },
    name: {
        type: DataTypes.STRING,
        allowNull: false,
    },
    email: {
        type: DataTypes.STRING,
        allowNull: false,
        unique: true,
    },
    password: {
        type: DataTypes.STRING,
        allowNull: false,
    },
    phone: {
        type: DataTypes.STRING,
        allowNull: true,
    },
    gender: {
        type: DataTypes.ENUM('male', 'female', 'other'),
        allowNull: true,
    },
    dob: {
        type: DataTypes.DATEONLY,
        allowNull: true,
    },
    is_active: {
        type: DataTypes.BOOLEAN,
        defaultValue: true,
    }
}, {
    tableName: 'users',
    timestamps: true,
});

module.exports = User;
````

### 3. Repository Layer (Data Access)

````javascript
// filepath: c:\Users\Anand\Documents\Laravel\dating-app-api\src\repositories\userRepository.js
const { User } = require('../models');

class UserRepository {
    async findAll() {
        return await User.findAll();
    }

    async findById(id) {
        return await User.findByPk(id);
    }

    async findByEmail(email) {
        return await User.findOne({ where: { email } });
    }

    async create(data) {
        return await User.create(data);
    }

    async update(id, data) {
        const user = await User.findByPk(id);
        if (!user) return null;
        return await user.update(data);
    }

    async delete(id) {
        const user = await User.findByPk(id);
        if (!user) return null;
        await user.destroy();
        return true;
    }
}

module.exports = new UserRepository();
````

### 4. Service Layer (Business Logic)

````javascript
// filepath: c:\Users\Anand\Documents\Laravel\dating-app-api\src\services\userService.js
const userRepository = require('../repositories/userRepository');
const bcrypt = require('bcryptjs');

class UserService {
    async getAllUsers() {
        const users = await userRepository.findAll();
        // Strip passwords before returning
        return users.map(u => {
            const { password, ...safe } = u.toJSON();
            return safe;
        });
    }

    async getUserById(id) {
        const user = await userRepository.findById(id);
        if (!user) throw new Error('User not found');
        const { password, ...safe } = user.toJSON();
        return safe;
    }

    async createUser(data) {
        // Business rule: check if email already exists
        const existing = await userRepository.findByEmail(data.email);
        if (existing) throw new Error('Email already registered');

        // Business rule: hash password
        const salt = await bcrypt.genSalt(10);
        data.password = await bcrypt.hash(data.password, salt);

        const user = await userRepository.create(data);
        const { password, ...safe } = user.toJSON();
        return safe;
    }

    async updateUser(id, data) {
        // Business rule: don't allow email change to existing email
        if (data.email) {
            const existing = await userRepository.findByEmail(data.email);
            if (existing && existing.id !== id) throw new Error('Email already in use');
        }

        const user = await userRepository.update(id, data);
        if (!user) throw new Error('User not found');
        const { password, ...safe } = user.toJSON();
        return safe;
    }

    async deleteUser(id) {
        const result = await userRepository.delete(id);
        if (!result) throw new Error('User not found');
        return { message: 'User deleted successfully' };
    }
}

module.exports = new UserService();
````

````javascript

const userService = require('../services/userService');
const apiResponse = require('../utils/apiResponse');

class UserController {
    async getAll(req, res) {
        try {
            const users = await userService.getAllUsers();
            return apiResponse.success(res, 'Users retrieved successfully', users);
        } catch (error) {
            return apiResponse.error(res, error.message);
        }
    }

    async getById(req, res) {
        try {
            const user = await userService.getUserById(req.params.id);
            return apiResponse.success(res, 'User retrieved successfully', user);
        } catch (error) {
            return apiResponse.error(res, error.message, 404);
        }
    }

    async create(req, res) {
        try {
            const user = await userService.createUser(req.body);
            return apiResponse.success(res, 'User created successfully', user, 201);
        } catch (error) {
            return apiResponse.error(res, error.message, 400);
        }
    }

    async update(req, res) {
        try {
            const user = await userService.updateUser(req.params.id, req.body);
            return apiResponse.success(res, 'User updated successfully', user);
        } catch (error) {
            return apiResponse.error(res, error.message, 400);
        }
    }

    async delete(req, res) {
        try {
            const result = await userService.deleteUser(req.params.id);
            return apiResponse.success(res, result.message);
        } catch (error) {
            return apiResponse.error(res, error.message, 400);
        }
    }
}

module.exports = new UserController();
````
